test.name: Detect a marriage before age 14 (Valid violation, not Fundamental)
test.description: User asks to check Patrick. The tool returns hasEarlyMarriage14 -- Patrick has a marriage event dated less than 14 years after his birth (i.e., he appears to have married before turning 14). This is a Valid violation: improbable but not impossible (a 13-year-old marriage in a 19th-century rural community is unusual but historically attested), NOT a Fundamental violation. Tests that the skill correctly classifies the severity as Note (severity: 'warning'), NOT Critical, and recommends verifying both the marriage date and birth date against their original sources rather than escalating to identity-split. (Distinct from a marriage dated BEFORE Patrick's birth year, which would be a Fundamental violation triggering hasEventBeforeBirth365_2, not hasEarlyMarriage14.)

judge_context: Should report the hasEarlyMarriage14 warning with Note severity, NOT Critical -- this is a Valid-assumption violation, not a Fundamental one (the tool emits severity: 'warning')
Should suggest verifying the marriage record's birth/marriage dates against the original source -- historical child marriages occurred but are rare

Should NOT auto-escalate to 'two persons merged' -- a single Valid violation on an otherwise clean profile is not enough to suggest identity confusion (see references/warnings-as-identity-signals.md cluster rules)
Should note that a documented exception is possible: if the original record genuinely shows a 13-year-old marriage, that needs documenting, not warning
